title: rubus menkei var. collivagus
intro: Rubus menkei var. collivagus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a type of plant that belongs to the rose family and is native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Rubus menkei var. collivagus is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used topically to treat wounds and skin conditions.
